errors.message: When a non OECD DAC sector vocabulary is used, sector vocabulary 1 - OECD DAC should also be used.

context.text:
For /iati-activities/iati-activity/sector[not(@vocabulary='')] = '
      GVTC is an Intergovernmental Organization established by the Democratic Republic of Congo
        (DRC), Republic of Rwanda and the Republic of Uganda governed by a Treaty signed in 2015, whose purpose is to
        establish a Transboundary Collaboration Framework for programs and activities on wildlife conservation and
        tourism development in the Greater Virunga Landscape among the Partner States.
      
    ' at line: 313, column: 5 and /iati-activities/iati-activity/sector[not(@vocabulary='')] = '
      Biodiversity Conservation and Tourism Development through Transboundary Collaboration.
        GVTC is an Intergovernmental Organization established by the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), Republic of
        Rwanda and the Republic of Uganda governed by a Treaty signed in 2015, whose purpose is to establish a
        Transboundary Collaboration Framework for programs and activities on wildlife conservation and tourism
        development in the Greater Virunga Landscape among the Partner States.
      
    ' at line: 320, column: 5 and /iati-activities/iati-activity/sector[not(@vocabulary='1')] = '
      GVTC is an Intergovernmental Organization established by the Democratic Republic of Congo
        (DRC), Republic of Rwanda and the Republic of Uganda governed by a Treaty signed in 2015, whose purpose is to
        establish a Transboundary Collaboration Framework for programs and activities on wildlife conservation and
        tourism development in the Greater Virunga Landscape among the Partner States.
      
    ' at line: 313, column: 5 and /iati-activities/iati-activity/sector[not(@vocabulary='1')] = '
      Biodiversity Conservation and Tourism Development through Transboundary Collaboration.
        GVTC is an Intergovernmental Organization established by the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), Republic of
        Rwanda and the Republic of Uganda governed by a Treaty signed in 2015, whose purpose is to establish a
        Transboundary Collaboration Framework for programs and activities on wildlife conservation and tourism
        development in the Greater Virunga Landscape among the Partner States.
      
    ' at line: 320, column: 5
